raw_user_request: Build an inventory restock dashboard for warehouse coordinators.
concrete_goal: Build a browser-based inventory restock dashboard for warehouse coordinators to add supplier reorder requests, filter by risk, and update restock status from the first screen.
success_criteria: Warehouse coordinators can add, edit, save, and filter supplier reorder requests without leaving the first screen.
The dashboard shows SKU, supplier, quantity, risk level, reorder status, validation, empty, loading, error, and saved-success states.
Desktop and mobile layouts keep restock cards, supplier filters, and status controls readable without overlap.
scope_in: Single-page inventory restock dashboard UI with local client-side state.
Seeded warehouse data for SKUs, suppliers, stock risk, quantities, and reorder statuses.
Responsive controls, accessible labels, keyboard focus, validation messages, and state views.
scope_out: Authentication, purchasing integrations, real supplier APIs, payments, and backend deployment.
assumptions: Fictional supplier and SKU data is acceptable for the prototype.
Local browser state is enough for the first usable version.
implementation_constraints: Do not add external supplier services or paid APIs.
Keep the primary restock workflow available on the first screen.

target_users: Warehouse coordinators who track SKU shortages, supplier requests, reorder risk, and restock readiness during daily inventory review.
product_brief.core_problem: Warehouse coordinators need a concise restock workspace so urgent supplier reorder requests and incomplete stock actions are not missed.
primary_workflows: Create or update a supplier restock request with SKU, supplier, quantity, risk level, due date, and reorder status.
Filter restock cards by high risk, supplier, and open status so coordinators can focus on urgent shortages.
domain_assumptions: Use warehouse vocabulary such as SKU, supplier, reorder request, risk level, quantity, due date, and restock status.
High-risk shortages and blocked reorder statuses must be visible before secondary notes.
content_data_model_assumptions: Primary entity: restock request with fields id, SKU, supplier, quantity, risk level, due date, owner, status, updated time, and notes.

Status values include draft, pending supplier, ordered, received, and blocked; UI states include empty queue, loading suppliers, validation errors, disabled invalid save, saved-success, and recoverable supplier error.
non_goals: No purchasing integration, supplier API connection, authentication, analytics, or backend persistence.

ux_workflow_completeness: Primary supplier restock workflow covers adding, filtering, and updating stock request records from the first screen.
visual_polish: Visual hierarchy distinguishes SKU, supplier, risk level, quantity, and reorder status without placeholder cards.
responsive_desktop_mobile: Desktop board and mobile stacked restock cards are inspected with concrete viewport evidence.
accessibility_basics: Controls have labels, visible focus, keyboard reachability, aria status text, and contrast for risk badges.
error_loading_empty_states: Empty, loading, validation, disabled, saved-success, and recoverable supplier error states are represented.
text_overlap_layout_stability: Long supplier names, SKU labels, and reorder notes wrap without overlap or clipping beside controls.
domain_fit: Labels, sample data, and interactions fit warehouse inventory restock work with SKU and supplier vocabulary.
evidence_requirements: Evidence must include desktop screenshot, mobile screenshot, behavior check, accessibility check, and state coverage artifact.

scenario_flows.name: Create urgent restock request
preconditions: The restock dashboard is loaded with fictional SKU data.
steps: Enter SKU, supplier, quantity, risk level, due date, and reorder status.
Save the request and confirm it appears in the high-risk queue.
scenario_flows.expected_result: The saved restock request is visible with supplier, quantity, risk badge, and status details.
failure_or_edge_cases: Missing SKU or supplier quantity shows validation and disables save.
Simulated supplier API error preserves the draft and shows retry guidance.

scenario_flows.name: Filter high-risk restocks
preconditions: At least one restock request is high risk and at least one is received.
steps: Open a restock card details area.
Update one request status and clear the filter.
scenario_flows.expected_result: Counts, card visibility, and reorder status labels update without hiding urgent shortages.
failure_or_edge_cases: Empty high-risk filter results show reset guidance.
Long supplier notes wrap on mobile without overlap.

verification_matrix.build: Run the repository build command when present.
verification_matrix.lint: Run lint or equivalent static checks when present.
verification_matrix.run: Start the local app or open the static artifact and report the URL or file path.
verification_matrix.behavior_check: Exercise create, filter, save, validation, empty, error, desktop, mobile, and keyboard/focus behavior.

acceptance_evidence_plan: Desktop and mobile screenshots plus behavior workflow, accessibility keyboard focus labels aria contrast, and empty loading error state coverage will be checked for the inventory restock dashboard.
iteration_policy: Rework until all app quality gates and evidence categories pass.

delegation_plan_seed.objective: Build the inventory restock dashboard app.
